bird.name: Wied's tyrant-manakin
bird.summary: Wied's tyrant-manakin (Neopelma aurifrons) is a Near-threatened species of bird in the family Pipridae, the manakins. It is endemic to Braz…

relations.subject: Wied's tyrant-manakin
relations.evidence: "Its lowland forest habitat has been historically threatened by agricultural conversion, deforestation for mining and plantation production
relations.object_summary: 原生林被砍伐导致栖息地面积和连通性下降。

relations.subject: Wied's tyrant-manakin
relations.evidence: Current key threats to these forests are urbanisation, agricultural expansion, selective logging, dam construction, colonisation and associated road building." Alterations in its habitat due to climate change is a further potential threat. It is considered rare to uncommon. "Conservation priorities include establishment of effective protection of the small population found NE of Rio de Janeiro city; field surveys also required in order to determine whether the species survives at other sites within its range."
relations.object_summary: 长期改变水文节律与生态系统稳定性的全球变化因素。

relations.object_summary: 种群规模过小导致遗传多样性下降和脆弱性上升。
